program_status.line: Phases 1 through 6 are built. The engine is now at the point where the next gain comes from choosing a path, not rebuilding the stack.
program_status.paragraph: The system has already mapped mechanisms, causal bridges, progression objects, translational packets, endotypes, and a ranked decision slate. Right now the slate holds 4 candidate decision(s), while Phase 4 remains bounded with 0 actionable packet(s).
program_status.current_direction_line: The engine is currently steering toward Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel because Future slates use this panel as the default operator readout set.

current_manuscript_candidate.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel
current_manuscript_candidate.story: Track OCLN, CLDN5, TJP1, Cerebral blood flow to test whether OCLN is moving the expected lane biology.

next_paper_opportunity.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel
next_paper_opportunity.story: Track OCLN, CLDN5, TJP1, Cerebral blood flow to test whether OCLN is moving the expected lane biology.

phases.role_line: Maps the recurring TBI mechanism lanes the rest of the engine builds on.
Shows which mechanisms are likely driving what happens next.
Tracks the recurring downstream damage states that matter over time.
Attaches targets, readouts, and intervention logic to the mechanism map.
Splits TBI into cohort and endotype patterns instead of one generic disease bucket.
Turns the structured engine into a ranked decision slate for the human operator.

major_findings: The lead mechanism in the live atlas is Mitochondrial Dysfunction.
The strongest supported bridge right now is BBB permeability increase -> peripheral immune infiltration.

The current lead insight is that Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel is the best readout set to carry the next paper story, because this panel is already attached to a translational packet and can be tested against 4 endotype packet(s).
current_unknowns: The top current uncertainty is Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed.
Phase 4 is still bounded. It has 6 bounded packet(s), 1 lane(s) with compound support, and 0 lane(s) with trial support.
The biggest paper blocker right now is this: Barrier readouts can improve in tissue without reducing leakage or downstream inflammatory spillover.

primary_decision.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel
primary_decision.human_question: Do you want this to become the default panel for the next readout pass?
primary_decision.why_now: This panel is already attached to a translational packet and can be tested against 4 endotype packet(s).
what_we_know: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure is currently rated supported.
It is tied to 1 Phase 2 transition(s) and 2 downstream object(s).
It already maps to 4 endotype packet(s).
what_is_uncertain: The main open issue is this: Barrier readouts can improve in tissue without reducing leakage or downstream inflammatory spillover.
This item still carries novelty status: Cross Disease Analog.
phase_path: Phase 1 mapped the live mechanism lane as Blood Brain Barrier Failure.
Phase 2 connected that lane through BBB Permeability Increase To Peripheral Immune Infiltration.
Phase 3 says the downstream burden shows up as Microglial Chronic Activation, Neurovascular Uncoupling.
Phase 4 attached the translational packet Blood Brain Barrier Failure.
Phase 5 says this matters most in Acute Mild Biomarker Imaging Bridge, Acute Severe Vascular Dominant, Acute Blast Vascular Inflammatory Mixed, and 1 more.
Phase 6 ranked Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel now because This panel is already attached to a translational packet and can be tested against 4 endotype packet(s)..

options.immediate_action: Refresh public enrichment and rebuild the decision surfaces around this panel.
options.future_effect: Future slates use this panel as the default operator readout set.

primary_decision.recommended_reason: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel already links mechanism, translation, and endotype context, so it is the strongest default panel to carry into the paper path.
primary_decision.current_manuscript_effect: If you choose Adopt as the default panel, Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel becomes part of the current manuscript path instead of staying only on the background slate.
primary_decision.future_engine_effect: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel will steer future machine runs and slates this way: Future slates use this panel as the default operator readout set.
primary_decision.statement: Track OCLN, CLDN5, TJP1, Cerebral blood flow to test whether OCLN is moving the expected lane biology.

source_row.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel
source_row.statement: Track OCLN, CLDN5, TJP1, Cerebral blood flow to test whether OCLN is moving the expected lane biology.
source_row.rationale: OCLN is the most evidence-dense tight-junction repair target in the current TBI corpus and best fits a barrier-module perturbation frame.
source_row.decision_rationale: OCLN is the most evidence-dense tight-junction repair target in the current TBI corpus and best fits a barrier-module perturbation frame.
source_row.why_now: This panel is already attached to a translational packet and can be tested against 4 endotype packet(s).
source_row.source_quality_mix: lane 74 full-text-like / 14 abstract-only; direct target mentions 6 full-text-like / 0 abstract-only
source_row.blockers: Barrier readouts can improve in tissue without reducing leakage or downstream inflammatory spillover; keep barrier-module claims tied to permeability outcomes, not junction markers alone.
source_row.next_test: Run an acute-to-subacute BBB repair packet around OCLN with Evans blue or gadolinium leakage, CLDN5/OCLN/TJP1 restoration, and a downstream inflammatory spillover panel.
source_row.unlocks: A cleaner panel lets Phase 4 readouts and Phase 5 endotype discrimination use the same operator-facing signal set.

secondary_decisions.title: Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed
secondary_decisions.decision_title: Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed
secondary_decisions.human_question: Do you want the next cycle to spend real effort resolving this uncertainty?
secondary_decisions.why_now: This endotype is already in the product, so clarifying it has immediate downstream value.
what_we_know: Tau / Proteinopathy Progression is currently rated provisional.
It is tied to 3 Phase 2 transition(s) and 3 downstream object(s).
It already maps to 1 endotype packet(s).
what_is_uncertain: The main open issue is this: Direct cohort-level ties between clearance readouts, tau variants, and longitudinal function are still sparse.
This item still carries novelty status: Cross Disease Analog.
phase_path: Phase 1 mapped the live mechanism lane as Tau Proteinopathy Progression, Glymphatic Astroglial Clearance Failure, Neuroinflammation Microglial State Change.
Phase 2 connected that lane through Glymphatic Failure To Tau Protein Accumulation, Neuroinflammation To Tau Proteinopathy Progression, Tau Proteinopathy Progression To Chronic Network Dysfunction.
Phase 3 says the downstream burden shows up as Tauopathy Progression, Cognitive Decline Phenotype, Microglial Chronic Activation.
Phase 4 attached the translational packet Tau Proteinopathy Progression, Glymphatic Astroglial Clearance Failure, Neuroinflammation Microglial State Change.
Phase 5 says this matters most in Chronic Repetitive Tau Clearance Mixed.
Phase 6 ranked Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed now because This endotype is already in the product, so clarifying it has immediate downstream value..

options.label: Resolve this now
options.immediate_action: Trigger targeted extraction when possible, otherwise run manual enrichment to resolve it.
options.future_effect: Future slates will keep this uncertainty at the front until it is clarified.
options.paper_effect: This clears a blocker to publication if the hinge resolves cleanly.

options.label: Monitor only
options.what_it_steers: Keep it visible without spending the next cycle here.
options.future_effect: The hinge stays on the board at lower urgency.
options.paper_effect: The blocker remains open for now.

options.label: Reject this split for now

options.future_effect: Future slates stop privileging this split unless new evidence reopens it.
options.paper_effect: This narrows scope, but may miss nuance.

secondary_decisions.recommended_reason: Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed is the main blocker on the current path, so resolving it now gives the cleanest gain in decision confidence.
secondary_decisions.current_manuscript_effect: If you choose Resolve this now, Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed becomes part of the current manuscript path instead of staying only on the background slate.
secondary_decisions.future_engine_effect: Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed will steer future machine runs and slates this way: Future slates will keep this uncertainty at the front until it is clarified.
secondary_decisions.statement: A chronic repetitive tau/clearance endotype may separate better by AQP4 or DTI-ALPS plus tau variants than by inflammation markers alone.

source_row.title: Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed
source_row.statement: A chronic repetitive tau/clearance endotype may separate better by AQP4 or DTI-ALPS plus tau variants than by inflammation markers alone.
source_row.rationale: p-tau or BD-tau together with AQP4 or DTI-ALPS-type clearance evidence.
source_row.decision_rationale: p-tau or BD-tau together with AQP4 or DTI-ALPS-type clearance evidence.
source_row.why_now: This endotype is already in the product, so clarifying it has immediate downstream value.
source_row.blockers: Direct cohort-level ties between clearance readouts, tau variants, and longitudinal function are still sparse.
source_row.next_test: Do chronic repetitive cohorts separate more cleanly by clearance failure plus tau than by inflammatory burden alone?
source_row.unlocks: A sharper endotype split improves biomarker ranking and target matching for Phase 4 packets.
source_row.weakness_reason: Direct cohort-level ties between clearance readouts, tau variants, and longitudinal function are still sparse.
source_row.needed_enrichment: Deepen repetitive-impact and CTE-adjacent papers with tau variants, glymphatic readouts, and chronic function.

source_row.title: Mitochondrial / Bioenergetic Collapse biomarker panel
source_row.statement: Track ATP, ROS, NfL, Bcl-2 to test whether PRKN is moving the expected lane biology.
source_row.rationale: PRKN is the cleanest current TBI-core mitochondrial perturbation target in the repo and already has a strong manual target packet.
source_row.decision_rationale: PRKN is the cleanest current TBI-core mitochondrial perturbation target in the repo and already has a strong manual target packet.
source_row.why_now: This panel is already attached to a translational packet and can be tested against 3 endotype packet(s).
source_row.source_quality_mix: lane 57 full-text-like / 11 abstract-only; direct target mentions 2 full-text-like / 0 abstract-only
source_row.blockers: Study found no significant correlation, contradicting the hypothesis of cumulative damage.; Oxidative-stress markers may improve without real ATP rescue or downstream inflammasome relief; keep this packet tied to both mitochondrial and inflammatory readouts.
source_row.next_test: Test a PRKN-centered rescue packet with ATP, ROS, apoptosis, and NLRP3 / IL-1beta readouts across the acute-to-subacute window.
source_row.unlocks: A cleaner panel lets Phase 4 readouts and Phase 5 endotype discrimination use the same operator-facing signal set.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 1 · Process Lanes
phase_timeline.summary: The engine reduced the TBI literature into six recurring process lanes so the project stopped treating every paper as a one-off mechanism note.
phase_timeline.detail: Today’s decisions only make sense because the lane map already tells us which biology is actually in play.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 2 · Causal Transitions
phase_timeline.summary: The engine converted lane-level mechanisms into explicit causal bridges, so the system can say what likely drives what next.
phase_timeline.detail: Current hinge and leverage decisions are built on these bridges.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 3 · Progression Objects
phase_timeline.summary: The engine grouped recurring downstream damage states into progression objects such as chronic network dysfunction and neurovascular uncoupling.
phase_timeline.detail: The current decision slate uses these objects to explain why a choice matters downstream.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 4 · Translational Logic
phase_timeline.summary: The engine attached translational packets to each lane so targets, readouts, and intervention logic sit on top of the mechanism map.
phase_timeline.detail: Biomarker panels and leverage-point choices come directly from these packets.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 5 · Cohort Stratification
phase_timeline.summary: The engine split TBI into cohort and endotype patterns so mild, repetitive, blast, severe, acute, and chronic states stop collapsing into one disease bucket.
phase_timeline.detail: The current ambiguity around tau versus clearance is an endotype problem, not just a mechanism problem.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 6 · Hypothesis Rankings
phase_timeline.summary: The engine ranked the strongest bridges, weakest hinges, biomarker panels, leverage points, and next tasks into a weekly decision slate.
phase_timeline.detail: The portal now uses this slate as the human decision queue.

dynamic extraction throttling and quota-aware backoff

journal.name: Journal of Neurotrauma
journal.notes: Strong home for TBI mechanism, biomarker, and translational synthesis if the evidence chain is explicit and bounded.

reasons: Audience overlap: Biomarker, Mechanism, Neurotrauma
Manuscript mode fit: Endotype Discriminator, Mechanistic Biomarker
Article-type overlap: original research, review
Family fit: journal often suits this manuscript family.
Article type is compatible with the captured journal requirements.
Current evidence chain meets the journal requirements snapshot.
Translation maturity is within the journal requirements bounds.
Scientific strength clears the minimum threshold recorded for this journal.

snapshot.notes: Manual capture from the official Journal of Neurotrauma instructions supplied on April 12, 2026. This journal explicitly considers original research, reviews, short communications, and letters to the editor; case reports are not accepted. It accepts format-neutral first submissions but still requires IRB/disclosure elements and a 500-word Transparency, Rigor, and Reproducibility Summary after the conclusion. Operational fit rule: mechanistic biomarker and pathophysiological studies can remain bounded in translational maturity if claims are explicitly limited and the evidence chain, declarations, and reproducibility summary are complete. Additional useful but non-blocking follow-up would be the linked article-type-specific rigor pages for pathophysiological mechanistic studies and fluid biomarker papers.

journal.name: Journal of Neurotrauma
journal.notes: Strong home for TBI mechanism, biomarker, and translational synthesis if the evidence chain is explicit and bounded.

reasons: Audience overlap: Biomarker, Mechanism, Neurotrauma
Manuscript mode fit: Endotype Discriminator, Mechanistic Biomarker
Article-type overlap: original research, review
Family fit: journal often suits this manuscript family.
Article type is compatible with the captured journal requirements.
Current evidence chain meets the journal requirements snapshot.
Translation maturity is within the journal requirements bounds.
Scientific strength clears the minimum threshold recorded for this journal.

snapshot.notes: Manual capture from the official Journal of Neurotrauma instructions supplied on April 12, 2026. This journal explicitly considers original research, reviews, short communications, and letters to the editor; case reports are not accepted. It accepts format-neutral first submissions but still requires IRB/disclosure elements and a 500-word Transparency, Rigor, and Reproducibility Summary after the conclusion. Operational fit rule: mechanistic biomarker and pathophysiological studies can remain bounded in translational maturity if claims are explicitly limited and the evidence chain, declarations, and reproducibility summary are complete. Additional useful but non-blocking follow-up would be the linked article-type-specific rigor pages for pathophysiological mechanistic studies and fluid biomarker papers.
